release_under_review.release_title: v0.14.0 — M15 Learning Sovereignty and Evidence-Bound Capability Memory

publication_evidence_inventory.release_title: v0.14.0 — M15 Learning Sovereignty and Evidence-Bound Capability Memory

tagged_readme_observation.stale_statement_summary: The maintained README still says tag and GitHub Release publication remain future human-controlled actions and does not claim they exist.

release_reference_audit.historical_reference_policy: Pre-publication contracts, fixtures, schemas, evaluators, and negative tests remain historical evidence and are not reinterpreted as current release state.

completion_approval_evidence.not_release_blocking_reason: The GitHub envelopes expose exact author, time, and ordering, and the approval body binds the candidate, verification receipt, and approved action; the gap is the absence of a standardized envelope-bound repository record.

rollback_and_correction.supersession_and_withdrawal_semantics: A correction preserves v0.14.0, records a successor commit, and uses an explicit patch release when release-level correction is appropriate; supersession or withdrawal must preserve discoverable historical evidence.

retrospective.summary: Decision Proof remained the governed decision record and evidence linkage stayed non-authoritative, but the runtime approval gate is not composed as one mandatory transition across learning and capability admission.
Learning readiness and authorization remain separated and non-authoritative; known-offset and expiry semantics need stricter temporal validation.
Capability memory remains evidence-bound and non-authorizing, while byte-level portability evidence and a canonical whole-pack digest remain future work.

The GitHub envelopes provide exact external actor, time, and ordering evidence, while repository JSON retains placeholder approval fields rather than a standardized envelope-bound observation; E4 also lacks timestamp ordering checks.
Maintained M15 evaluators remain deterministic, offline, caller-data-only, non-mutating, and non-authoritative, with fail-closed behavior for malformed evidence.

Repository completion and publication remained separate and the release is reproducible, while README state, technical immutability enforcement, historical non-movement proof, and the generic release-proof schema need follow-up.

findings.title: README retains pre-publication wording after v0.14.0 publication
findings.summary: The release entry and M1-M15 completion state are present, but Next Phase still says the tag and GitHub Release are not claimed to exist.
findings.recommendation: Update the maintained README to state that v0.14.0 is published while preserving the separate human-authorization boundary for M16.

findings.title: Published release and lightweight tag lack recorded technical immutability enforcement
findings.summary: The tag resolves correctly, but it is lightweight and the GitHub Release API reports immutable=false; immutability is a governance policy rather than an evidenced technical control.
findings.recommendation: Add protected-tag or ruleset enforcement and retain an audit-log receipt for future releases without moving or recreating v0.14.0.

findings.title: Point-in-time ref evidence cannot prove the tag was never moved
findings.summary: Current remote and clean-clone observations agree, but no retained historical audit event stream proves that refs/tags/v0.14.0 was never moved or recreated before observation.
findings.recommendation: Human review may accept this bounded historical limitation for v0.14.0 and require durable tag audit evidence prospectively.

findings.title: Published human-approval payload retains template placeholders
findings.summary: PR #253 comment 5021955084 retains placeholder JSON fields even though its GitHub envelope supplies exact actor aa-os and timestamp 2026-07-20T11:57:17Z; the approval body still binds the candidate, receipt, and approved action.
findings.recommendation: Standardize envelope-bound record validation for actor, timestamp, candidate, tree, receipt, and action without replacing the GitHub envelope.

findings.title: E4 evaluator validates timestamp shape but not event ordering
findings.summary: The final-completion evaluator accepts a Z-suffixed approval timestamp but does not prove observation, verification, approval, merge, and publication ordering.
findings.recommendation: Add strict RFC 3339 parsing and monotonic event-order tests in a successor change.

findings.title: Learning Proof temporal validation admits unknown local offset and does not evaluate current expiry
findings.summary: The parser accepts -00:00 and verifies expiration ordering, but evaluation has no supplied observation time against which an otherwise valid proof can be declared expired.
findings.recommendation: Reject -00:00 and add caller-supplied, explicitly bound evaluation time for expiry decisions without using ambient time.

findings.title: Decision Proof approval-gate composition is not mandatory across learning and capability admission
findings.summary: Individual Decision Proof, Learning Proof, and Capability Memory controls preserve authority, but no single contract requires runtime approval-gate evidence across the complete admission-to-use transition.
findings.recommendation: Specify a future cross-control admission transition that binds Decision Proof approval evidence without allowing it to grant learning, invocation, or execution authority by itself.

findings.title: Generic release-proof schema is too shallow for post-release evidence
findings.summary: The generic schema records tag presence and summary consistency but not exact commit/tree binding, Latest identity, notes and asset digests, clean-clone reproduction, or classified retrospective findings.
findings.recommendation: Consider a future versioned, reusable post-release evidence-package contract based on this release-specific record.

findings.title: Tag resolves to the exact reviewed merge commit
findings.summary: refs/tags/v0.14.0 and its peeled commit resolve to 01870f4b844c1cda2f157e7be7bdb66317fdc738, not the PR head.

findings.title: GitHub Release metadata and authority wording are consistent
findings.summary: Release 356696715 uses v0.14.0, is published, non-draft, non-prerelease, Latest, identifies M15, and contains no observed authority overreach.
findings.recommendation: Do not edit or republish the existing release.

findings.title: Completion PR and issue states are consistent
findings.summary: PR #253 is merged, #252 and #231 are closed as completed after merge, and #254 remains open for human review.
findings.recommendation: Keep Issue #254 open until the human exit decision is recorded.

findings.title: Tagged release is independently reproducible
findings.summary: A fresh remote clone resolved the expected commit and tree, remained clean, and ran 2275 maintained tests successfully without repository-local hidden state.
findings.recommendation: Retain the exact command and result as the v0.14.0 verification baseline.

findings.title: Learning and capability evidence remain non-authorizing
findings.summary: Learning Proof and Capability Memory maintain separate evidence, lifecycle, and authority semantics and do not grant invocation or execution authority.

findings.title: M15 runtime evaluators preserve inert fail-closed boundaries
findings.summary: The maintained evaluators inspect caller-supplied evidence without network, provider, lifecycle, release, approval, or mutation authority.
findings.recommendation: Keep future evaluators caller-data-only and deterministic.

findings.title: Rollback and correction semantics preserve historical release evidence
findings.summary: The recorded policy forbids moving v0.14.0 and requires successor commits plus an explicit patch release where release-level correction is appropriate.
findings.recommendation: Apply these semantics to any future correction; perform no rollback or release mutation in this review.

findings.title: Capability Memory lacks commit-external byte verification and a canonical whole-pack digest
findings.summary: The pack evaluator compares declared internal digests but does not hash referenced bytes or expose a canonical digest over the complete Capability Memory record.
findings.recommendation: Add commit-external byte-verification receipts and a canonical whole-record digest before operational consumption; the evidence must remain non-sealing and non-executing.

m16_planning_recommendation.rationale: No release-blocking defect was observed. The classified non-blocking follow-ups do not prevent planning review, but only a separate explicit human decision may authorize M16 planning; implementation remains out of scope.

authority_boundary: This post-release record and its evaluator are evidence only. They do not move, delete, or recreate v0.14.0; edit or republish the GitHub Release; close Issue #254; authorize M16 planning or implementation; seal Decision Proof or Learning Proof; grant capability invocation or execution authority; accept risk; execute rollback; close an audit; grant a waiver; or transfer governance authority.
runtime_boundary: The evaluator accepts one inert caller-supplied mapping and performs no file, Git, GitHub, network, subprocess, command, clone, test, tag, release, issue, or external-state operation. It calculates only an in-memory canonical SHA-256 over the supplied mapping to pin this release-specific record; it does not verify external artifact bytes.
